intent_type=intent_type,\n44\t        session_id=str(uuid4()),\n45\t        context=FetchContext(\n46\t            project_slug=\"test-project\",\n47\t            namespace=\"test-ns\",\n48\t        ),\n49\t    )\n50\t\n51\t\n52\tdef _slow_executor(delay: float):\n53\t    \"\"\"Executor that sleeps for `delay` seconds before returning success.\"\"\"\n54\t    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n55\t        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n56\t        return {\"ok\": True, \"delay\": delay}\n57\t    return _fn\n58\t\n59\t\n60\tdef _timeout_after(delay: float):\n61\t    \"\"\"Executor that blocks longer than a typical timeout to test timeout enforcement.\"\"\"\n62\t    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n63\t        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n64\t        return {\"ok\": True}\n65\t    return _fn\n66\t\n67\t\n68\tdef _error_executor(error: Exception):\n69\t    \"\"\"Executor that raises `error`.\"\"\"\n70\t    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n71\t        raise error\n72\t    return _fn\n73\t\n74\t\n75\tdef _quick_executor(source: FetchSource):\n76\t    \"\"\"Executor that returns success quickly with source info.\"\"\"\n77\t    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n78\t        # Small delay to simulate fast but not instant execution\n79\t        await asyncio.sleep(0.01)\n80\t        return {\"ok\": True, \"source\": source.value}\n81\t    return _fn\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass TestFetchWindowPolicy:\n85\t    \"\"\"Tests for the fetch-window policy gating synthesis.\"\"\"\n86\t\n87\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n88\t    async def test_synthesis_waits_for_all_sources_to_complete(self):\n89\t        \"\"\"Synthesis fires only after all sources complete (window close), not earlier.\"\"\"\n90\t        from src.fetch.commands import FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n91\t\n92\t        strand = FetchStrand()\n93\t\n94\t        # Get the actual sources for STATUS intent\n95\t        status_sources = [spec.source for spec in _build_request(IntentType.STATUS)\n103\t\n104\t        # Track when sources complete vs when fetch completes\n105\t        source_completion_times = []\n106\t        window_close_time = policy acceptance tests (bead adc-3ib6).\n\nTests that synthesis gates on window close, per-source timeouts, progress states,\nand timing metrics are properly recorded.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- Synthesize fires exactly once per thread when all sources have resolved or their\n  per-source timeouts expire\n- Late/timed-out sources appear only as fetch_coverage caveats — never re-synthesis\n- During the window, per-source progress states stream to the pending card ('3/5 sources in')\n- fetch_first_source_ms and fetch_total_ms (window close) are recorded in dispatch_timings\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.fetch.commands import (\n    FetchCommandSpec,\n    FetchContext,\n    FetchRequest,\n    FetchSource,\n    IntentType,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchStrand\nfrom src.instrument.timings import DispatchTimings\nfrom src.intent.router import IntentRouter, RoutedIntent, IntentClassification\nfrom src.sse.broadcaster import SSEBroadcaster, SSEEvent\n\n\n# --- test helpers -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _build_request(intent_type: IntentType = IntentType.STATUS) -> FetchRequest:\n    \"\"\"Create a test fetch request.\"\"\"\n    return FetchRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid4()),\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        session_id=str(uuid4()),\n        context=FetchContext(\n            project_slug=\"test-project\",\n            namespace=\"test-ns\",\n        ),\n    )\n\n\ndef _slow_executor(delay: float):\n    \"\"\"Executor that sleeps for `delay` seconds before returning success.\"\"\"\n    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n        return {\"ok\": True, \"delay\": delay}\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _timeout_after(delay: float):\n    \"\"\"Executor that blocks longer than a typical timeout to test timeout enforcement.\"\"\"\n    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n        return {\"ok\": True}\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _error_executor(error: Exception):\n    \"\"\"Executor that raises `error`.\"\"\"\n    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        raise error\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _quick_executor(source: FetchSource):\n    \"\"\"Executor that returns success quickly with source info.\"\"\"\n    async def _fn(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        # Small delay to simulate fast but not instant execution\n        await asyncio.sleep(0.01)\n        return {\"ok\": True, \"source\": source.value}\n    return _fn\n\n\nclass TestFetchWindowPolicy:\n    \"\"\"Tests for the fetch-window policy gating synthesis.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_synthesis_waits_for_all_sources_to_complete(self):\n        \"\"\"Synthesis fires only after all sources complete (window close), not earlier.\"\"\"\n        from src.fetch.commands import FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # Get the actual sources for STATUS intent\n        status_sources = [spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS]]\n\n        # Stub only the sources that STATUS uses with different delays\n        delays = {source: 0.05 + (i * 0.02) for i, source in enumerate(status_sources)}\n        for source, delay in delays.items():\n            strand._source_executors[source] = _slow_executor(delay)\n\n        request = _build_request(IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        # Track when sources complete vs when fetch completes\n        source_completion_times = []\n        window_close_time = None\n\n        def on_progress(source, result):\n            source_completion_times.append(time.monotonic())\n\n        start = time.monotonic()\n        result = await strand.fetch(request, on_partial_result=on_progress)\n        window_close_time = time.monotonic() - start\n\n        # All sources should have completed\n        assert len(source_completion_times) == len(status_sources)\n\n        # Window close should happen after the slowest source completes\n        # (within a small margin for async overhead)\n        max_delay = max(delays.values())\n        assert window_close_time >= max_delay * 0.9  # Allow 10% timing variance\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_slow_source_times_out_does_not_block_window_close(self):\n        \"\"\"A source slower than its timeout should not delay synthesize past window close.\"\"\"\n        from src.fetch.commands import FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # Make one source very slow (2s)\n        slow_source = FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS\n        strand._source_executors[slow_source] = _timeout_after(2.0)\n\n        # Other sources complete quickly\n        status_sources = [spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS]]\n        for source in status_sources:\n            if source != slow_source:\n                strand._source_executors[source] = _quick_executor(source)\n\n        request = _build_request(IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        # Override the command spec to use a short timeout for the slow source\n        original_commands = FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS]\n        modified_commands = []\n        for spec in original_commands:\n            if spec.source == slow_source:\n                modified_commands.append(FetchCommandSpec(\n                    source=spec.source,\n                    command_template=spec.command_template,\n                    timeout_seconds=0.1,  # Short timeout\n                    required=spec.required,\n                    cacheable=spec.cacheable,\n                ))\n            else:\n                modified_commands.append(spec)\n\n        # Mock both get_fetch_commands and get_source_timeout_ms to ensure the short timeout is used\n        # (config/fetch.yaml may have a longer timeout that would override the spec)\n        with patch('src.fetch.orchestrator.get_fetch_commands') as mock_commands, \\\n             patch('src.fetch.commands.get_source_timeout_ms', return_value=None):\n            mock_commands.return_value = modified_commands\n\n            start = time.monotonic()\n            result = await strand.fetch(request)\n            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start\n\n        # Window should close around the timeout, not the full 2s\n        # (timeout is 0.1s, allow overhead for other sources)\n        assert elapsed < 1.0, f\"Window took {elapsed:.3f}s, should have timed out at 0.1s\"\n\n        # The timed-out source should be marked correctly\n        assert slow_source in result.coverage.timed_out\n        assert result.sources[slow_source].status == \"timeout\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_progress_events_fire_as_sources_complete(self):\n        \"\"\"Per-source progress states should stream to pending card during the window.\"\"\"\n        from src.fetch.commands import FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # Get a subset of sources for testing\n        test_sources = [FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS, FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP, FetchSource.GIT_LOG]\n\n        # Use staggered delays to ensure sources complete in a known order\n        delays = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: 0.05,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: 0.10,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: 0.15,\n        }\n        for source, delay in delays.items():\n            strand._source_executors[source] = _slow_executor(delay)\n\n        # Stub other sources to succeed immediately\n        status_sources = [spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS]]\n        for source in status_sources:\n            if source not in delays:\n                strand._source_executors[source] = _quick_executor(source)\n\n        request = _build_request(IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        # Track progress events\n        progress_events = []\n        completed_order = []\n\n        def on_progress(source, result):\n            completed_order.append(source)\n            progress_events.append({\n                \"source\": source,\n                \"status\": result.status,\n                \"completed_count\": len(completed_order),\n            })\n\n        await strand.fetch(request, on_partial_result=on_progress)\n\n        # Should have received progress events for all sources\n        assert len(completed_order) == len(status_sources)\n\n        # The test sources should complete in delay order (fastest first)\n        test_completions = [s for s in completed_order if s in test_sources]\n        assert test_completions[0] == FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS\n        assert test_completions[1] == FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP\n        assert test_completions[2] == FetchSource.GIT_LOG\n\n        # Progress events should have correct incremental counts\n        test_events = [e for e in progress_events if e[\"source\"] in test_sources]\n        assert test_events[0][\"completed_count\"] >= 1\n        assert test_events[1][\"completed_count\"] >= 2\n        assert test_events[2][\"completed_count\"] >= 3\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_timed_out_sources_included_in_caveats(self):\n        \"\"\"Sources that time out should appear in caveats and not trigger re-synthesis.\"\"\"\n        from       for source in status_sources:\n            if source != timeout_source:\n                strand._source_executors[source] = _quick_executor(source)\n\n        request =        patch('src.fetch.commands.get_source_timeout_ms', return_value=None):\n            mock_commands.return_value = modified_commands\n\n            result = await strand.fetch(request)\n\n        # Timed-out source should be in the caveats\n        assert result.caveats is not None\n        assert any(\"timed out\" in caveat.lower() for caveat in result.caveats)\n\n        # Should be in timed_out bucket\n        assert timeout_source in result.coverage.timed_out\n\n        # Result should still be returned (synthesis can proceed with caveats)\n        assert result is not None\n\n\nclass TestFetchWindowTimingMetrics:\n    \"\"\"Tests for fetch_first_source_ms and fetch_total_ms timing recording.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_fetch_first_source_ms_recorded(self):\n        \"\"\"fetch_first_source_ms should record time to first source resolution.\"\"\"\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # Create a simple test      }\n\n        request = _build_request(IntentType.STATUS)\n\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        fetch_start = timings.clock()\n        first_source_at = [None]\n        first_source_name Load urgency classification rules from prompts/urgency.md (hot-reload).\n\n        urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py. Parse JSON response\n            result_data = json.loads(raw)\n\n            # Extract fields\n            data = result_data.get(\"data\", {})\n            summary = result_data.get(\"summary\", \"\")\n            urgency_str = result_data.get(\"urgency\", request.urgency)\n\n            # Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data={\"type\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to parse synthesis response\"},\n                summary=\"An error occurred while processing the result.\",\n                urgency=Urgency.NORMAL,\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Synthesis failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_user_message(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build user message for LLM call.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"## Intent Specification\",\n            f\"Intent Type: {request.intent_type.value}\",\n            f\"Project: {request.project_slug or 'none'}\",\n            f\"Urgency: {request.urgency}\",\n            f\"\",\n            f\"## Utterance\",\n            f\"{request.utterance}\",\n            f\"\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.fetched_context:\n            lines.append(\"## Fetched Context\")\n\n            # Add results from each source\n            for source, source_result in request.fetched_context.sources.items():\n                lines.append(f\"\\n### {source.value}\")\n                if source_result.status == \"success\":\n                    # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global synthesize strand instance\n_synthesize_strand: Optional[SynthesizeStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_synthesize_strand(prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global synthesize strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _synthesize_strand\n    if _synthesize_strand is None:\n        _synthesize_strand = SynthesizeStrand(prompt_path=prompt_path)\n    return _synthesize_strand\n\n\nasync def synthesize_intent(request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to synthesize an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: SynthesizeRequest\n\n    Returns:\n        SynthesizeResult\n    \"\"\"\n    strand = get_synthesize_strand()\n    return await strand.synthesize(request)\n","numLines":267,"startLine":1,"totalLines":267}}}

\"\"\"\n50\t\n51\t    def __init__(self):\n52\t        self._source_executors = {\n53\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n54\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS:    f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n96\t            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n97\t        )\n98\t\n99\t        # Create tasks for concurrent (success/timeout/error)\n116\t        sources = {}\n117\t        succeeded = []\n118\t        timed_out = []\n119\t        failed = []\n120\t        skipped = []\n121\t        caveats = []\n122\t\n123\t        # Wrap each task with its timeout and error handling\n124\t        # This allows true concurrent waiting with per-source timeouts\n125\t        async def execute_with_timeout(\n126\t            source: FetchSource,\n127\t            required: bool,\n128\t            timeout: int | float,\n129\t            task: asyncio.Task\n130\t        ) ->                )\n154\t                return source, required, result\n155\t\n156\t        # Create wrapped tasks for concurrent execution\n157\t        wrapped_tasks = [\n158\t )\n197\t\n198\t        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n199\t\n200\t        result = FetchResult(\n201\t            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n202\t resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n406\t        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n407\t        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n408\t        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n409\t        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n410\t        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n411\t        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n412\t\n413\t        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n414\t        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n415\t        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n416\t        carrying the reason. container_statuses if cs.get(\"ready\", False))\n967\t        total_count = len(container_statuses)\n968\t\n969\t        return f\"{ready_count}/{total_count}\"\n970\t\n971\t\n972\tclass FetchOrchestrator:\n973\t    \"\"\"\n974\t    Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n975\t\n976\t    This is a convenience wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n977\t    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n978\t\n979\t    Features:\n980\t    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n981\t    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n982\t    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n983\t    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n984\t    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n985\t    \"\"\"\n986\t\n987\t    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n988\t        \"\"\"\n989\t        Initialize the orchestrator.\n990\t\n991\t        Args:\n992\t            fetch_strand: FetchStrand instance (uses global if not provided)\n993\t        \"\"\"\n994\t        if fetch_strand is None:\n995\t            self._strand = FetchStrand()\n996\t        else:\n997\t            self._strand = fetch_strand\n998\t\n999\t    async def execute_fetch(\n1000\t        self,\n1001\t        request: FetchRequest,\n1002\t        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n1003\t    ) -> FetchResult:\n1004\t        \"\"\"\n1005\t        Execute a fetch request with concurrent source execution.\n1006\t\n1007\t        Args:\n1008\t            request: The fetch request with intent type and context\n1009\t            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n1010\t\n1011\t        Returns:\n1012\t            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n1013\t        \"\"\"\n1014\t        logger.info(\n1015\t            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n1016\t            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n1017\t        )\n1018\t\n1019\t        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n1020\t        # - Concurrent execution\n1021\t        # - Per-source timeouts\n1022\t        # - Streaming callbacks\n1023\t        # - Coverage tracking\n1024\t        result = await self._strand.fetch(\n1025\t            request=request,\n1026\t            on_partial_result=stream_callback,\n1027\t        )\n1028\t\n1029\t        logger.info(\n1030\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n1031\t            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n1032\t            f\"{result.total_duration_ms}ms\"\n1033\t        )\n1034\t\n1035\t        return result\n1036\t\n1037\t\n1038\t# Global orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport get_fetch_commands,\n    get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom .clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, resolve_argocd_endpoint\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            # Get effective timeout from config file or spec default\n            effective_timeout = get_effective_timeout(spec, request.context.project_slug)\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, effective_timeout, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete with per-source timeout enforcement\n        # We use asyncio.wait with ALL_COMPLETED to detect window close:\n        # - All tasks run concurrently\n        # - Each task is wrapped to handle its own timeout\n        # - As tasks complete, we process them immediately\n        # - Window closes when ALL tasks reach terminal state (success/timeout/error)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        # Wrap each task with its timeout and error handling\n        # This allows true concurrent waiting with per-source timeouts\n        async def execute_with_timeout(\n            source: FetchSource,\n            required: bool,\n            timeout: int | float,\n            task: asyncio.Task\n        ) -> tuple[FetchSource, bool, SourceResult]:\n            \"\"\"Execute a task with timeout, returning (source, required, result).\"\"\"\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n                return source, required, result\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n\n        # Create wrapped tasks for concurrent execution\n        wrapped_tasks = [\n            execute_with_timeout(source, required, timeout, task)\n            for source, required, timeout, task in tasks\n        ]\n\n        # Wait for all wrapped tasks to complete concurrently\n        # This is the WINDOW CLOSE detection: we wait until ALL tasks finish\n        completed_results = await asyncio.gather(*wrapped_tasks)\n\n        # Process results as they arrive (maintaining completion order)\n        for source, required, result in completed_results:\n            # Stream partial result immediately (progressive window fill)\n            if on_partial_result:\n                on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n            sources[source] = result\n\n            if result.status == \"success\":\n                succeeded.append(source)\n                # Check if the source data contains a caveat (e.g., fallback path with narrower scope)\n                if isinstance(result.data, dict) and \"caveat\" in result.data:\n                    caveats.append(result.data[\"caveat\"])\n            elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n            elif result.status == \"error\":\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                else:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n            )\n            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n                  })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\n\n        The ArgoCD endpoint is resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n\n        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n        \"\"\"\n        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n        if not app_name:\n            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n\n        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n                cluster=context.cluster,\n            )\n\n        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n\n                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n list) else 0,\n                        \"scope\": \"project_workspace\",  # Primary path\n                    }\n                else:\n                    error_output = stderr.decode().strip() or stdout.decode().strip()\n                    if \"no beads workspace\" in error_output or \"No such file\" in error_output:\n                        # Fallback path: no local .beads workspace at {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b09282f1f944474aa491ae38","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"Per-stage timing capture helpers for dispatch instrumentation.\n2\t\n3\t``DispatchTimings`` is a small accumulator that records stage durations in\n4\tmilliseconds during a single dispatch. It is passed through the router\n5\t(``route_utterance`` → ``process_intent`` → the fetch/synthesize/escalate\n6\tbranches); each stage records the duration it measures, and ``process_intent``\n7\tpersists one ``dispatch_timings`` row per intent thread via\n8\t``SessionStore.record_dispatch_timings(**timings.to_fields())``.\n9\t\n10\tDesign notes:\n11\t\n12\t- The clock is injectable (default ``time.monotonic``) so tests get\n13\t  deterministic durations without sleeping.\n14\t- Only stages actually measured appear in ``to_fields()``; unmeasured stages\n15\t  are simply absent (the store upsert leaves them NULL), which is the honest\n16\t  representation — e.g. ``synthesize_first_token_ms`` cannot be measured until\n17\t  the synthesize strand streams, and ``escalate_ms`` is absent for hot-path\n18\t  dispatches.\n19\t- ``percentiles`` uses the nearest-rank method (ceil(q/100 * n)), matching how\n20\t  latency SLOs are typically reported. SQLite has no built-in percentile, so\n21\t  the store fetches ordered per-stage values and calls into here.\n22\t\"\"\"\n23\t\n24\timport time\n25\tfrom typing import Callable, Optional\n26\t\n27\t\n28\tClock = Callable[[], float]\n29\t\n30\t# The timing stages tracked per dispatch. Mirrors the dispatch_timings columns\n31\t# (minus intent_id/created_at). Kept here as the canonical list the collector\n32\t# accepts so a typo in a stage name is a KeyError, not a silent no-op.\n33\tDISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES = (\n34\t    \"router_ms\",\n35\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n36\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n37\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n38\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n39\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n40\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n41\t    \"stt_ms\",\n42\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n43\t)\n44\t\n45\t\n46\tclass DispatchTimings:\n47\t    \"\"\"Accumulates per-stage millisecond durations for one dispatch.\n48\t\n49\t    Usage in the router::\n50\t\n51\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n52\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n53\t\n54\t        fetch_start = timings.clock()\n55\t        ...\n56\t        timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n57\t\n58\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t\n61\t    def __init__(self, clock: Clock = time.monotonic):\n62\t        self._clock = clock\n63\t        self._durations: dict[str, int] = {}\n64\t\n65\t    @property\n66\t    def clock(self) -> Clock:\n67\t        \"\"\"The monotonic clock callable (exposed so callers share one clock).\"\"\"\n68\t        return self._clock\n69\t\n70\t    def record(self, stage: str, ms: int | float | None) -> None:\n71\t        \"\"\"Record a stage duration in ms. Returns ``{q: value}``. Raises\n96\t    ``ValueError`` on an empty list — callers should skip empty stages rather\n97\t    than call this (the store does so).\n98\t    \"\"\"\n99\t    if not values:\n100\t        raise ValueError(\"percentiles() requires at least one value\")\n101\t    ordered = sorted(values)\n102\t    n = len(ordered)\n103\t    out: dict[int, int] = {}\n104\t    for q in qs:\n105\t        if not 0 <= q <= 100:\n106\t            raise ValueError(f\"percentile q must be in [0, 100], got {q}\")\n107\t        idx = (q * n + 99) // 100 - 1  # ceil(q/100 * n) - 1\n108\t        idx = max(0, min(n - 1, idx))\n109\t        out[q] = ordered[idx]\n110\t    return out\n111\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"49776d2f-1944-4a9f-9671-ba87acc41bd3","uuid":"165803b3-97a8-48ae-8d2f-d863b020c2d9","timestamp":"2026-07-24T01:47:23.831Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/instrument/timings.py","content":"\"\"\"Per-stage timing capture helpers for dispatch instrumentation.\n\n``DispatchTimings`` is a small accumulator that records stage durations in\nmilliseconds during a single dispatch. It is passed through the router\n(``route_utterance`` → ``process_intent`` → the fetch/synthesize/escalate\nbranches); each stage records the duration it measures, and ``process_intent``\npersists one ``dispatch_timings`` row per intent thread via\n``SessionStore.record_dispatch_timings(**timings.to_fields())``.\n\nDesign notes:\n\n- The clock is injectable (default ``time.monotonic``) so tests get\n  deterministic durations without sleeping.\n- Only stages actually measured appear in ``to_fields()``; unmeasured stages\n  are simply absent (the store upsert leaves them NULL), which is the honest\n  representation — e.g. ``synthesize_first_token_ms`` cannot be measured until\n  the synthesize strand streams, and ``escalate_ms`` is absent for hot-path\n  dispatches.\n- ``percentiles`` uses the nearest-rank method (ceil(q/100 * n)), matching how\n  latency SLOs are typically reported. SQLite has no built-in percentile, so\n  the store fetches ordered per-stage values and calls into here.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport time\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\n\n\nClock = Callable[[], float]\n\n# The timing stages tracked per dispatch. Mirrors the dispatch_timings columns\n# (minus intent_id/created_at). Kept here as the canonical list the collector\n# accepts so a typo in a stage name is a KeyError, not a silent no-op.\nDISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n\nclass DispatchTimings:\n    \"\"\"Accumulates per-stage millisecond durations for one dispatch.\n\n    Usage in the router::\n\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        fetch_start = timings.clock()\n        ...\n        timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, clock: Clock = time.monotonic):\n        self._clock = clock\n        self._durations: dict[str, int] = {}\n\n    @property\n    def clock(self) -> Clock:\n        \"\"\"The monotonic clock callable (exposed so callers share one clock).\"\"\"\n        return self._clock\n\n    def record(self, stage: str, ms: int | float | None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a stage duration in ms. None is ignored (stage stays absent).\"\"\"\n        if ms is None:\n            return\n        if stage not in DISPATCH_TIMING_STAGES:\n            raise KeyError(f\"Unknown dispatch timing stage: {stage!r}\")\n        self._durations[stage] = int(ms)\n\n    def elapsed_ms(self, start: float, end: float | None = None) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Milliseconds between ``start`` and ``end`` (default: now), via the clock.\"\"\"\n        return int(((end if end is not None else self._clock()) - start) * 1000)\n\n    def to_fields(self) -> dict[str, int]:\n        \"\"\"Return the recorded stages as a kwargs dict for record_dispatch_timings.\"\"\"\n        return dict(self._durations)\n\n    def get(self, stage: str) -> Optional[int]:\n        \"\"\"Return a recorded stage duration, or None if unmeasured.\"\"\"\n        return self._durations.get(stage)\n\n\ndef percentiles(values: list[int], qs: tuple[int, ...] = (50, 95)) -> dict[int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Nearest-rank percentiles for a list of integer samples.\n\n    Each ``q`` (0..100) maps to ``values[ceil(q/100 * n) - 1]`` after sorting\n    ascending, clamped to ``[0, n-1]``. Returns ``{q: value}``. Raises\n    ``ValueError`` on an empty list — callers should skip empty stages rather\n    than call this (the store does so).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not values:\n        raise ValueError(\"percentiles() requires at least one value\")\n    ordered = sorted(values)\n    n = len(ordered)\n    out: dict[int, int] = {}\n    for q in qs:\n        if not 0 <= q <= 100:\n            raise ValueError(f\"percentile q must be in [0, 100], got {q}\")\n        idx = (q * n + 99) // 100 - 1  # ceil(q/100 * n) - 1\n        idx = max(0, min(n - 1, idx))\n        out[q] = ordered[idx]\n    return out\n","numLines":111,"startLine":1,"totalLines":111}}}

Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n327\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n328\t\n329\t    async def route_utterance(\n330\t        self,\n331\t        utterance: str,\n332\t        utterance_id: str,\n333\t        session_id: str,\n334\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n335\t        \"\"\"\n336\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n337\t\n338\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n339\t\n340\t        Args:\n341\t            utterance: The user utterance\n342\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n343\t            session_id: Session ID\n344\t\n345\t        Returns:\n346\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n347\t\n348\t        Raises:\n349\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n350\t        \"\"\"\n351\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n352\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n353\t\n354\t        try:\n355\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n356\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n357\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n358\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n359\t            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n360\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n361\t\n362\t            routed_intents = []\n363\t            for classification in classifications:\n364\t                # Create intent ID\n365\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n366\t\n367\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n368\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n369\t                    classification=classification,\n370\t                    session_id=session_id,\n371\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n372\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n373\t                )\n374\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n375\t\n376\t            return routed_intents\n377\t\n378\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n379\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n380\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n381\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n382\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n383\t                utterance=utterance,\n384\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n385\t                session_id=session_id,\n386\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n387\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n388\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n389\t            )\n390\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n391\t\n392\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n393\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n394\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n395\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n396\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n397\t                utterance=utterance,\n398\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n399\t                session_id=session_id,\n400\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n401\t            )\n402\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n403\t\n404\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n405\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n406\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n407\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n408\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n409\t                utterance=utterance,\n410\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n411\t                session_id=session_id,\n412\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n413\t            )\n414\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n415\t\n416\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n417\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n418\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n419\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n420\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n421\t                utterance=utterance,\n422\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n423\t                session_id=session_id,\n424\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n425\t            )\n426\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n427\t\n428\t    async def process_intent(\n429\t        self,\n430\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n431\t    ) -> dict:\n432\t        \"\"\"\n433\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n434\t\n435\t        Args:\n436\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n437\t\n438\t        Returns:\n439\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n440\t        \"\"\"\n441\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n442\t\n443\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n444\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n445\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n446\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n447\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n448\t        # the dispatch itself.\n449\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n450\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n451\t\n452\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n453\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n454\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n455\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n456\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n457\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n458\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n459\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n460\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n461\t            )\n462\t\n463\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n464\t            store = await self._get_store()\n465\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n466\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n467\t                \"resolved\",\n468\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n469\t            )\n470\t\n471\t            return {\n472\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n473\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n474\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n475\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n476\t            }\n477\t\n478\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n479\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n480\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n481\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n482\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n483\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n484\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n485\t            )\n486\t\n487\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n488\t            store = await self._get_store()\n489\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n490\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n491\t                \"resolved\",\n492\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n493\t            )\n494\t\n495\t            return {\n496\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n497\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n498\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n499\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n500\t            }\n501\t\n502\t        try:\n503\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n504\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n505\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n506\t            else:\n507\t                # Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n523\t        try:\n524\t            store = await self._get_store()\n525\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n526\t        except Exception as e:\n527\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n528\t\n529\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n530\t        self,\n531\t )\n584\t\n585\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n586\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n587\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n588\t source\n602\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n603\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n604\t            fetch_start =           # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n612\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n613\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n614\t\n615\t                # Increment completed count\n616\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n617\t\n618\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n619\t        ))\n627\t\n628\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n629\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n630\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n631\t                timings.record(\n632\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n633\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, failed_sources.append({\n648\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n649\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n650\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n651\t                    })\n652\t\n653\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n654\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n655\t                await               \"failed\",\n668\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n669\t                )\n670\t\n671\t                return {\n672\t                    \"intent_id\":        # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n721\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n722\t                logger.error(\n723\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n724\t                )\n725\t\n726\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n727\t                await FetchIntentType:\n879\t        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n880\t\n881\t        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n882\t        \"\"\"\n883\t        # Map enum values by string\n884\t        type_map = {\n885\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n886\t            IntentType.ACTION: exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE
